name: composition_advisor
purpose: JSON knowledge base + decision rules consulted before generating MIDI patterns. Encodes music theory primitives, genre-specific conventions, energy/frequency-space allocations, silence protocols, and conditional 'good->WOW' branches drawn from observed failure modes of prior attempts (Faust, Nemesis, Bramure, Venin).

core_principles.rule: A drop only hits if the bar before STARVES the ear. Build hunger before payoff.
core_principles.concrete_test: Last bar before Drop must have ≥ 1 element (drum, bass, or harmony) drop out OR thin to single track for ≥ 2 beats.

core_principles.rule: Silence is the 4th compositional dimension after pitch/duration/timbre. Plant it deliberately, not by omission.
core_principles.concrete_test: Every section ≥ 8 bars contains at least one PLANNED silence (sub-bar to multi-bar) at a structural pivot.

core_principles.rule: A Final Drop is more memorable when it RESTATES something from earlier (motif, single note, rhythmic idea) than when it adds new layers.
core_principles.concrete_test: Final Drop must contain ≥ 1 callback (intro_drone, verse_motif octave-up, bridge_chord_resolution, or rhythmic quote).

core_principles.rule: Each section carries exactly ONE compositional idea (rhythmic OR melodic OR harmonic OR textural). Stacking ideas crowds; isolating one makes it audible.
core_principles.concrete_test: Identify each section's PRIMARY idea in 1 sentence. If multiple compete, simplify.

core_principles.rule: When ≥ 2 instruments share a critical band, exactly ONE leads; the other yields (-6 dB OR pan ≥ 40% off-center OR sidechain duck).
core_principles.concrete_test: Audit by band: at most 1 dominant per [sub, low, low-mid, mid, high-mid, high, air].

core_principles.rule: Velocity curves > velocity steps. Timing offsets ±1/64 humanize. Pitch wobble (±10 cents) adds desperate/menacing quality. Mathematically perfect = sterile.
core_principles.concrete_test: No pattern should use a single velocity value across all hits of one instrument in one section.

philosophy.good_to_wow_meta: Each conditional branch in this API encodes ONE specific lift from 'sounds OK' to 'memorable'. Apply branches conservatively — one well-executed WOW per section beats five competing ones.

voicing_recipes.i_sus2: root + 2 + 5 — leaves the b3 implicit, dark but unresolved
voicing_recipes.VI_open: b6 + 1 + b3 — wide open, classic Aeolian
voicing_recipes.VII_to_i: VII major resolves down a whole step to i — the Aeolian cadence

aeolian.characteristic_dissonance: VII_to_i (Aeolian cadence is itself the tension — feels modal, not Classical-tonal)

chord_families.ii: minor (the natural 6 lives here as the chord's 5)
chord_families.IV: major (b3, 6, 1) — the SIGNATURE Dorian chord, lifted by raised-6

voicing_recipes.i_to_IV: the i->IV move is the Dorian payoff — the raised 6 is suddenly audible
voicing_recipes.i_add6: root + b3 + 5 + 6 — Dorian without committing to chord IV

use_for: verse that wants relief without resolution
dorian.characteristic_dissonance: natural 6 against b3 (a major 4th interval that shouldn't exist in pure minor) — the Dorian raised-6 tension

signature_intervals: b2 (the half-step ABOVE the root — primary tension)

chord_families.bII: major (b2, 4, b6) — the SIGNATURE Phrygian chord, tritone-laden push from i

voicing_recipes.i_with_b9: root + b3 + 5 + b9 (the b2 pitched up an octave) — root + immediate dissonance
voicing_recipes.i_to_bII: the half-step UP move from i to bII is the Phrygian signature — sounds Spanish/flamenco/menacing
voicing_recipes.i_sus_b2: root + b2 + 5 — sus voicing where the b2 is the suspension

phrygian.characteristic_dissonance: b2 against root = minor 2nd, the primary musical 'wrongness' interval. Use sparingly or mode loses identity.

phrygian_dominant.scale_name: Phrygian Dominant (5th mode of harmonic minor)
signature_intervals: MAJOR 3 (the maj-3 over b2 = aug-2 interval = snake-charmer)

chord_families.I: major (1, 3, 5) — surprise major chord on the dark mode

voicing_recipes.I_dominant7_no5: root + maj3 + b7 — the venom voicing, no 5 lets the maj3-vs-b7 tritone dominate
voicing_recipes.I_sus4: root + 4 + 5 — avoid committing the maj-3 (lets bass deliver it)

phrygian_dominant.characteristic_dissonance: aug-2 interval between b2 and maj-3 (D to F nat in C# Phr Dom) — the unmistakable signature.

signature_intervals: b5 (the diminished 5th — root chord is i°, NO stable consonance)

chord_families.i_dim: diminished — the root chord itself is unstable

voicing_recipes.i_dim_no5: root + b3 — pseudo-stable by hiding the b5
voicing_recipes.i_with_b5: root + b3 + b5 — full diminished, maximum instability

locrian.characteristic_dissonance: Locrian has NO stable tonic — the root is itself a tritone away from the b5. Treat as a passing flavor, not a home key. Used wholesale = listener loses anchor.
locrian.warning: Per user preference (less dissonance), prefer Phrygian or Phrygian Dominant instead of Locrian for most use cases.

signature_intervals: natural 7 (leading tone — this is what makes it 'harmonic')

chord_families.V: MAJOR (5, 7, 2) — the SIGNATURE chord, contains the leading tone — Classical V-i cadence

voicing_recipes.V_to_i: the dominant V to tonic i resolution — feels classical but with the b6 lurking
voicing_recipes.i_add_nat7: root + b3 + 5 + nat7 — minor-major 7 chord, very brooding
voicing_recipes.iv_to_V_to_i: ii->V->i but with both ii dim and V major — the harmonic minor cadence

avoid_for: modal (use Aeolian/Dorian instead) — Harmonic Minor pulls toward classical resolution
harmonic_minor.characteristic_dissonance: the aug-2 between b6 and nat-7 — exoticism that resolves cleanly via the leading tone.

_TBD_D3: genres (Cobain, Reznor, Yorke, Daft Punk) + per-genre conditional rules

default_personality_blend._note: Sum = 100. More Daft Punk than Yorke per user preference. Each genre's >=25% threshold triggers its conditional rules (D3).
